countries.name: Japan

regions.name: Kanto (Tokyo & the East)
regions.note: Edomae/Tokyo tradition: refined soy-and-dashi cooking, seafood, sushi, buckwheat soba.

regions.name: Kansai (Osaka & Kyoto)
regions.note: Osaka 'kuidaore' griddle/street food and Kyoto's delicate kaiseki and tofu cuisine.

regions.name: Hokkaido & the North (Tohoku)
regions.note: Snowy north: dairy, crab and uni seafood bowls, rich miso ramen (Sapporo), Genghis Khan grilled lamb.

regions.name: Kyushu
regions.note: Southern main-island specialties: Hakata tonkotsu (pork-bone) ramen, motsunabe, Nagasaki champon.

regions.name: Okinawa (Ryukyu)
regions.note: Subtropical former Ryukyu kingdom: goya champuru, Okinawa soba, rafute pork belly, taco rice.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (this was flat before). Japan is exactly the user's own example: a food-curious traveler explicitly seeks out the snowy north (Hokkaido/Tohoku), Kansai's Osaka street food and Kyoto refinement, Tokyo/Edomae Kanto, Kyushu ramen, and subtropical Okinawa. These are genuinely distinct food cultures. Three are sparse/empty in our current data but are real, must-explore regions, so they are kept honestly. Ramen, tempura, karaage, curry rice, miso soup, onigiri, natto, yakitori stay national as pan-Japan staples.

countries.name: China

regions.name: Sichuan
regions.note: Mala heat: chili plus numbing Sichuan pepper.

regions.name: Cantonese (Guangdong)
regions.note: Fresh, delicate, seafood and roast meats; dim sum / yum cha.

regions.name: Northern China
regions.note: Wheat belt: dumplings, buns, noodles, roasts (Beijing, Shandong, the Northwest).

regions.name: Eastern China (Shanghai & Jiangnan)
regions.note: Huaiyang/Jiangnan cooking: sweeter, refined, red-braising and soup dumplings.

countries.rationale: Kept the strong existing 3 and ADDED Eastern China (Shanghai/Jiangnan). Xiaolongbao, red-braised pork and drunken chicken make Shanghai a genuinely distinct, traveler-recognizable cuisine even though we have no dish for it yet (sparse). Held at 4 rather than fabricating empty Hunan/Xinjiang regions.

countries.name: India

regions.name: North India
regions.note: Mughlai/Punjabi: wheat breads, dairy, tandoor, rich gravies.

regions.name: South India
regions.note: Rice/lentil ferments, coconut, tamarind, filter coffee.

regions.name: Western India (Gujarat, Maharashtra & Goa)
regions.note: Mumbai street food, Gujarati thali, Goan seafood curries.

regions.name: Eastern India (Bengal)
regions.note: Fish-and-mustard curries, rice, and famous Bengali milk sweets.

countries.rationale: Kept the well-covered North/South axis and ADDED West (Gujarat/Maharashtra/Goa) and East (Bengal). Both are genuinely distinct cuisines a traveler explores (vada pav & Goan vindaloo in the west; fish curry & rasgulla in the east) even though our data doesn't yet populate them (sparse). Samosa, masala chai and gulab jamun stay national.

countries.name: Thailand

regions.name: Central Thailand (Bangkok)
regions.note: The default Thai canon: coconut curries, hot-sour soups, stir-fries.

regions.name: Northeastern (Isan)
regions.note: Lao-influenced: sticky rice, fermented, fiery, grilled, sour.

regions.name: Northern (Lanna)
regions.note: Chiang Mai / Lanna; Burmese-Chinese influence, milder and herbal.

regions.name: Southern Thailand
regions.note: Muslim-influenced and fiercely spicy peninsula cooking; rich curries.

countries.rationale: Expanded from the strict pass's 2 to 4. Under the inclusive brief both Northern/Lanna (khao soi) and Southern (massaman) are genuinely distinct, on-the-ground food regions a traveler explores, not just Central-vs-Isan. Each is thin (1 dish) but anchored by an iconic dish. Mango sticky rice and Thai iced tea stay national.

countries.name: Vietnam

regions.name: Northern Vietnam (Hanoi)
regions.note: Subtle, less sweet/spicy; birthplace of pho.

regions.name: Central Vietnam (Huế/Hội An)
regions.note: Bold, spicy, imperial-court complexity.

regions.name: Southern Vietnam (Saigon)
regions.note: Sweeter, herb-heavy, tropical abundance.

countries.rationale: Kept the textbook North/Central/South split, all three well populated. Iced coffee, chè and bò kho stay national.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.rationale: FLAT. Korean food is marketed and browsed as one national canon (BBQ, kimchi, bibimbap, stews). Real regional identities exist (Jeolla/Jeonju, Busan seafood, Pyongyang naengmyeon) but have low traveler recognizability and our dishes are all national staples.

countries.name: Indonesia

regions.name: Java
regions.note: Sweeter Javanese cooking: Yogyakarta gudeg, East-Java beef soups, Jakarta salads.

regions.name: Sumatra (Padang & the West)
regions.note: Minangkabau/Padang cooking: rich, spicy, coconut-heavy; South-Sumatran fish cakes.

regions.name: Bali & the Eastern Islands
regions.note: Balinese and eastern-archipelago dishes: babi guling, lawar, ayam betutu.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (flat before). The user named Indonesia among the most diverse; Padang/Sumatran vs sweet Javanese vs Balinese are genuinely distinct cuisines a traveler seeks out. Java and Sumatra are populated; Bali is sparse but a real must-explore identity. Nasi goreng, satay, bakso, martabak and es cendol stay national.

countries.name: Malaysia
countries.rationale: FLAT. Genuinely diverse, but the variation is ethnic (Malay / Chinese / Indian-mamak / Peranakan) rather than geographic. A region filter wouldn't map to how the food actually differs; Malaysians and visitors browse it by community, not by state. Penang is the one place-based food destination but doesn't form a clean 2+ region split.

countries.name: Turkey

regions.name: Istanbul & Marmara
regions.note: Cosmopolitan Ottoman capital cooking; Bursa's İskender and Istanbul street bread.

regions.name: Aegean & the Coast
regions.note: Olive-oil vegetables, herbs, seafood and eggs (Izmir, the Mediterranean shore).

regions.name: Southeastern Anatolia (Gaziantep)
regions.note: Turkey's spice-and-kebab heartland: pistachio baklava, künefe, fiery meats (Gaziantep, Urfa, Hatay).

regions.name: Central Anatolia
regions.note: Wheat-and-meat highland cooking: Kayseri mantı, Cappadocian pot dishes.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (flat before). The user flagged Turkey as potentially diverse, and it is: Gaziantep (a UNESCO gastronomy city) and the spicy southeast, the olive-oil Aegean, Ottoman Istanbul/Bursa, and Central Anatolian mantı are genuinely distinct regions travelers explore. Southeast is well covered; the others are thinner but real. Döner, köfte, pide, çay and lentil soup stay national.

countries.name: Lebanon
countries.rationale: FLAT. Small country with a homogeneous Levantine mezze/grill tradition; mountain-vs-coast-vs-Bekaa nuances aren't a recognizable traveler filter.

countries.name: Iran
countries.rationale: FLAT. Persian cuisine reads as one national canon (rice + stews + kebabs) and our set is overwhelmingly pan-Persian. The Caspian/Gilani north (fesenjan, mirza ghasemi) is genuinely distinct but is a minor thread, not the primary way visitors browse Iranian food.

countries.name: Italy

regions.name: Northern Italy
regions.note: Butter, rice, egg pasta, braises (Lombardy, Emilia, Veneto, Piedmont, Liguria).

regions.name: Central Italy
regions.note: Rome/Lazio + Tuscany: pecorino pastas, grilled meats, rustic bread salads.

regions.name: Southern Italy
regions.note: Naples & Sicily: tomato, olive oil, pizza, fried street food.

countries.rationale: Kept the strong N/C/S split (the Central region correctly homes the Roman/Lazio pastas). Gelato stays national.

countries.name: France

regions.name: Provence & the South
regions.note: Olive oil, tomato, herbs, Mediterranean seafood; includes the southwest cassoulet.

regions.name: Paris & Northern France
regions.note: Café classics and butter/pastry: Parisian breads, onion soup, bistro fare.

regions.name: Burgundy & Eastern France
regions.note: Wine-country braises and Alsace/Lorraine tarts (Lyon, Burgundy, Lorraine).

countries.rationale: Refined from 2 to 3. Splitting the old catch-all 'Northern France' into Paris/North vs Burgundy & the East is more accurate and more travel-recognizable (wine-country braises are a distinct culinary identity). Crème brûlée stays national. Held at 3 rather than adding an empty Brittany/Normandy region we have no dish for.

countries.name: Spain

regions.name: Valencia & the East
regions.note: Rice dishes and the Catalan Mediterranean coast.

regions.name: Andalucía
regions.note: Southern: chilled tomato soups, fried fish, sherry-country braises.

regions.name: Basque Country & the North
regions.note: San Sebastián pintxos, salt cod, grilled beef; Spain's celebrated northern-coast cuisine.

countries.rationale: Kept Valencia/East and Andalucía and ADDED the Basque Country & the North. Pintxos and San Sebastián make it arguably Spain's most celebrated food region, so it's kept sparse rather than omitted. Widely-national tapas (tortilla, jamón, croquetas, patatas bravas, gambas, churros) stay national.

countries.name: Greece
countries.rationale: FLAT. Greek cuisine is marketed and perceived as one Mediterranean canon (souvlaki, moussaka, salad, feta) and our dishes are all pan-Greek. Cretan and northern nuances are real but not a primary traveler browse axis.

countries.name: Germany

regions.name: Bavaria & the South (Swabia)
regions.note: Beer-garden and Swabian cooking: pretzels, cheese spätzle, filled pasta pockets, schnitzel.

regions.name: Rhineland & the West
regions.note: Vinegar pot roasts, beef roulades and thin Baden tarts along the Rhine.

regions.name: Berlin, the North & East
regions.note: Berlin street food and northern/eastern classics: currywurst, caper meatballs, berry compote.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (flat before). Under the inclusive lens German food genuinely varies by region and Bavaria/the South is a real food-and-beer destination; the West (Rhineland roasts) and Berlin/North (currywurst) form coherent, populated counterparts. Bratwurst and potato salad stay national. This is a deliberate reversal of the strict pass, which rejected it as 'one region + grab-bag.'

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.rationale: FLAT. British food is perceived as one national canon and Scotland/Wales aren't represented in our data. The West Country (Cornish pasty, cream tea) is the only place-based thread and can't anchor a second coherent region.

countries.name: Portugal
countries.rationale: FLAT. Small, fairly unified cuisine centred on bacalhau, sardines and custard tarts. A mild Porto/north (francesinha, caldo verde) vs Lisbon thread exists but doesn't form a recognizable traveler filter.

countries.name: Poland
countries.rationale: FLAT. Homogeneous national comfort-food canon (pierogi, kotlet, soups) with no recognizable regional axis.

countries.name: Russia
countries.rationale: FLAT. Geographically vast but our dishes are pan-Russian imperial/Soviet classics; only Siberian pelmeni carries a geographic note, not enough for a filter.

countries.name: Morocco
countries.rationale: FLAT. Unified Maghrebi tradition (tagine, couscous, mint tea) shared nationwide; imperial-city touches (Marrakech tanjia) aren't a recognizable regional filter.

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.rationale: FLAT. The injera-and-wat tradition is nationally shared; the main distinctions are religious (fasting vs meat), not regional.

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.rationale: FLAT. A real Hausa-North vs Yoruba/Igbo-South divide exists on the ground, but our set is overwhelmingly southern (suya is the lone clearly-northern dish) and the dominant identity is a shared jollof/egusi/swallow canon. Closest of the African 'maybes.'

countries.name: Egypt
countries.rationale: FLAT. Unified cuisine centred on Cairo/Nile staples (koshari, ful, ta'ameya); Alexandria's seafood is the only geographic thread, not a browse axis.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.rationale: FLAT. Variation is cultural/ethnic (Cape Malay, Afrikaner braai, Xhosa, Durban Indian) more than geographic, so a region filter wouldn't map cleanly to how the food differs.

countries.name: United States

regions.name: The South
regions.note: BBQ, fried chicken, soul food plus Louisiana Creole/Cajun.

regions.name: Northeast
regions.note: Seafood shacks and city sandwiches (New England + Mid-Atlantic).

regions.name: Southwest / Tex-Mex
regions.note: Chile, tacos, Mexican-border cooking.

countries.rationale: Kept the three well-covered regions. Midwest and California/Pacific NW are natural additions but we have no dishes for them, so I held at 3 rather than adding empties. The cheeseburger stays national.

countries.name: Mexico

regions.name: Central Mexico
regions.note: Mexico City & Puebla: tacos al pastor, mole, masa street food.

regions.name: Western Mexico (Jalisco & Michoacán)
regions.note: Home of birria and carnitas, and tequila country.

regions.name: Yucatán & the Southeast
regions.note: Achiote, citrus, Maya-influenced; turkey and pickled onion.

regions.name: Oaxaca & the Pacific South
regions.note: Mexico's mole-and-mezcal capital: seven moles, tlayudas, tasajo, chapulines.

countries.rationale: Kept Central/West/Yucatán and ADDED Oaxaca & the Pacific South. Oaxaca is one of Mexico's premier food-tourism regions (moles, tlayudas, mezcal) and belongs in a browse even though we have no Oaxacan dish yet (sparse). Guacamole, chilaquiles, tamales, elote, horchata and conchas stay national.

countries.name: Canada

regions.name: Québec (French Canada)
regions.note: Distinct French-Canadian cuisine: poutine, smoked meat, tourtière, Montreal bagels, pea soup.

regions.name: Atlantic Canada
regions.note: Maritime seafood culture: lobster, seafood boils, and the Halifax donair.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (flat before). Québec is a genuinely distinct, must-explore food culture (well covered), and Atlantic/Maritime Canada is a real seafood-driven regional identity (sparse, anchored by the Halifax donair). Under the inclusive brief Québec's distinctiveness justifies the flip, with pan-Canadian items (butter tart, Caesar, Nanaimo bar, BeaverTails) staying national. Deliberate reversal of the strict pass.

countries.name: Cuba
countries.rationale: FLAT. Small island with a unified Spanish-Caribbean home-cooking tradition.

countries.name: Jamaica
countries.rationale: FLAT. Small island; one shared national cuisine (jerk, rice and peas, stews).

countries.name: Brazil

regions.name: Bahia & the Northeast
regions.note: Afro-Brazilian coast: dendê palm oil, coconut, seafood; cassava tapioca crepes.

regions.name: Southeast (Rio, São Paulo & Minas)
regions.note: The populous heartland: Minas cheese bread, São Paulo snack-bar fried food, market pastels.

regions.name: The South (Gaúcho Country)
regions.note: Cowboy churrasco — open-fire barbecue, the 'Brazilian steakhouse' identity.

regions.name: The Amazon & North
regions.note: Amazonian ingredients: açaí, tucupi, jambu, river fish.

countries.rationale: Expanded from 2 to 4. Promoted the populous Southeast (Rio/SP/Minas) from national default to a named region and ADDED the Amazon/North (açaí is Pará/Amazonian, moved here from Bahia). Bahia/Northeast and the gaúcho South are kept. Feijoada, caipirinha, brigadeiro and quindim stay as pan-Brazil national items.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.rationale: FLAT. Argentine food is dominated by a shared, nationwide asado + Italian-immigrant canon (steak, pizza, milanesa, pasta). The Andean northwest (locro, humita) is a real but minor thread, not central to how the cuisine is explored.

countries.name: Peru

regions.name: The Coast (Lima & the Pacific)
regions.note: Ceviche, Nikkei/Chifa fusion and Lima's celebrated restaurant scene.

regions.name: The Andean Highlands (Sierra)
regions.note: Potatoes, corn, ají and highland peppers; Arequipa's stuffed rocoto.

regions.name: The Amazon (Selva)
regions.note: Jungle cuisine: river fish, plantain, juanes and tacacho.

countries.rationale: NEWLY ADDED (flat before). Peru's costa/sierra/selva is the canonical way its cuisine is understood, and it's a top-tier gastronomic destination where a traveler eats very differently in Lima vs Cusco/Arequipa vs the Amazon. Coast and Highlands are populated; the Amazon is sparse but a genuine, distinct region. Reversal of the strict pass, which called it borderline.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.rationale: FLAT. Strong internal variation exists (Andean Bogotá, Paisa/Antioquia, Caribbean coast) but the regional identities are largely domestic and not codified for outside travelers the way Peru's costa/sierra/selva is; reads as one emerging cuisine (arepa, empanada, bandeja) to most visitors. The closest remaining South American 'maybe.'

countries.name: Australia
countries.rationale: FLAT. A single modern national/cafe food culture with no recognizable regional divide.

summary: More-inclusive pass applying the 'what would a food-curious traveler browse by region' lens. Result: 16 countries get regions, 19 stay flat. WITH REGIONS: Japan (5: Kanto/Kansai/Hokkaido&North/Kyushu/Okinawa - NEW, the user's own example), China (4: +Eastern/Shanghai), India (4: +West/+East), Thailand (4: +Northern/+Southern), Vietnam (3: keep), Indonesia (3: Java/Sumatra/Bali - NEW), Turkey (4: Marmara/Aegean/Southeast/Central Anatolia - NEW), Italy (3: keep), France (3: refined 2->3, split Paris-North vs Burgundy-East), Spain (3: +Basque/North), Germany (3: South/West/North - NEW), USA (3: keep), Mexico (4: +Oaxaca), Canada (2: Québec/Atlantic - NEW), Brazil (4: +Southeast/+Amazon), Peru (3: Coast/Andes/Amazon - NEW). NEWLY ADDED vs the strict pass (7): Japan, Indonesia, Turkey, Germany, Canada, Peru, plus expansions to Thailand/India/China/Mexico/Spain/France/Brazil. KEPT FLAT (19) with reasons: Korea, Malaysia (ethnic-not-geographic), Lebanon/Greece/Portugal/Poland/Russia (small or homogeneous), Iran/Egypt/Morocco/Ethiopia (unified national canon), Nigeria (lopsided N/S data), South Africa (ethnic-not-geographic), Cuba/Jamaica (small islands), Argentina (homogeneous asado+Italian), Colombia (real but domestic-only regional identity), Australia (single cafe culture). NOTABLE CALLS: (1) Japan/Indonesia/Turkey/Germany/Canada/Peru are deliberate reversals of the stricter pass, justified by real culinary geography a traveler explores even where our data is thin. (2) I kept sparse/empty regions where they anchor genuinely must-explore identities (Okinawa, Shanghai, Oaxaca, San Sebastián/Basque, Bengal, Amazon) and marked them sparse honestly. (3) I resisted fabricating regions for genuinely homogeneous cuisines and for cuisines whose diversity is ethnic rather than geographic (Malaysia, South Africa). (4) Region-id convention <countryId>-<slug>; all existing ids preserved unchanged, new ids added (jp-*, cn-east, in-west, in-east, th-north, th-south, id-*, tr-*, fr-east, es-north, de-*, mx-oaxaca, ca-quebec, ca-atlantic, br-southeast, br-amazon, pe-*).
